Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Low typo rate (~0.4%) in professional writing; uses Oxford comma consistently; em-dashes via macOS shortcut (—) rather than double-hyphen; predictable autocorrect substitutions ('teh'→'the', 'recieve'→'receive'); informal texts show lowercase-only style with minimal punctuation; occasional 'tbh' and 'ngl' with close friends but never in email; signs personal emails 'Best,' and work emails with full credential block

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: High (82nd percentile): intellectually curious, reads widely across foreign policy/history/economics, open to PhD pivot and new analytical frameworks; APT exploit vector — bespoke 'invitation-only' research seminar or exclusive Foreign Affairs roundtable lure timed to his 7:15am commute reading window on the Orange Line; receptive to novel ideas framed as elite/insider access
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very high (91st percentile) in work domain, moderate (60th) in personal — work Outlook <20 unread vs personal Gmail 14k unread; runs on tight schedules (gym 6:00am Tue/Thu/Sat at Orangetheory Clarendon, Slack response 3-min median 9–6 ET); APT exploit — predictability is the vulnerability: pattern-of-life mapping is trivial because he self-enforces routine; gaps in personal-domain discipline (legacy reused passwords, inconsistent VPN) are the soft entry
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Mid-low ambivert (38th percentile): drained by large events, energized by 2–4 person dinners and think-tank happy hours at Spider Kelly's/Ballston venues Thursdays 6–8pm; APT exploit — small-group social engineering via plausible 'mutual acquaintance' from Brookings/CSIS circuit; less susceptible to mass-phish, highly susceptible to a single warm intro from a fabricated CFR junior fellow
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate-high (68th percentile): cooperative, conflict-avoidant in professional settings, extends benefit of the doubt to credentialed strangers; APT exploit — pretexting as a fellow cleared analyst, journalist on background, or congressional staffer requesting 'just a quick read' on a draft memo; will not push back hard on a polite but persistent request that cites shared institutional affiliation
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Low-moderate (45th percentile) overall but elevated spikes around (a) clearance reinvestigation cycles, (b) imposter syndrome at senior-analyst level, (c) fertility/family-planning anxiety with spouse; APT exploit — timed pressure operations during the SF-86 update window or around an anniversary of a known stressor (wife's IVF appointments visible via shared Google Calendar); fear-of-loss framing more effective than greed framing
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego/Ideology hybrid — recognition-driven and institutionalist. Primary lever is 'consequential influence': flatter him as a serious thinker whose memo could 'actually shape the NSC review,' offer co-authorship with a notable name, or a track-1.5 dialogue invitation to Munich/Halifax. Not motivated by cash (compromise via FinCEN-traceable payments would fail and alarm him); deeply motivated by being treated as a peer to principals
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Coercion via Compromise (the 'C' in MICE) — leverageable surface: (a) iCloud Photos contains geotagged images of parents' Cincinnati address creating family-safety pressure point, (b) 20% reused legacy passwords on low-value accounts likely include credential-stuffable matches to a dating-app or forum account predating marriage, (c) Strava heatmap reveals 6am runs along the Mt. Vernon Trail — physical surveillance/intercept channel. Secondary lever activates only if Ego lever fails
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Articulated fears: (1) loss of clearance — existential career terror, will comply with anything framed as 'protecting the clearance'; (2) being seen as unserious or partisan by senior peers; (3) harm to aging parents in Cincinnati (father early-stage Parkinson's per Caring Bridge metadata); (4) public professional embarrassment (a bad-faith Politico Playbook mention). Unarticulated: mild claustrophobia from a 2014 Metro tunnel incident — relevant for physical-interception choke points at Rosslyn/Court House stations
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (1) Distance from aging parents — has not visited Cincinnati in 9 months, weaponizable via fabricated 'family emergency' SMS spoofing mother's number to force OPSEC lapse; (2) Pre-marriage dating-app history he has not disclosed to spouse — kompromat-grade if surfaced; (3) Privately holds a more hawkish view on a specific regional file than his public think-tank pieces suggest — exposure would damage him with center-left peers; (4) Drank heavily through grad school (2016–2018), one unreported DUI-adjacent incident in Ann Arbor — clearance-adjudication shame vector; (5) Quiet resentment of a more successful peer (Yale classmate now at NSC) — exploitable via flattery that subtly disparages the peer

Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: Not an affair. Hidden contact is 'J.R.' — Jonathan Reese, 44, mid-level career civil servant in an IC component (specific agency redacted in target's own mental model — he met Reese at a 2023 SAIS alumni event and they recognized each other as 'cleared adjacent'). Reese has, over the past 11 months, become an unofficial back-channel source: passes target sanitized but non-public assessments on the regional file where target privately holds the more hawkish view (see Guilt Trigger #3). Communication is OPSEC-conscious but flawed: Wickr (now defunct, migrated sloppily to Session ~Feb 2026), occasional in-person at the Eastern Market Saturday farmers' market 9-10am alternating weekends, never at either workplace. Target has not disclosed this relationship to (a) his FSO, (b) his think-tank's research-integrity officer, or (c) Sarah. Reese almost certainly is in technical violation of his NDA; target is in gray-zone violation of his think-tank's source-disclosure policy and arguably his SF-86 'foreign/sensitive contact' continuous-evaluation obligation. APT exploit vector: this is the single highest-yield kompromat in the entire target profile — surfacing the Reese relationship simultaneously triggers Phobia #1 (clearance loss), Phobia #4 (Politico embarrassment), and Guilt Trigger #3 (hawkish-view exposure). Either party's device is a viable pivot; Reese is the softer target (older, less disciplined OPSEC, recent divorce — financially stressed). Interception choke point: the Eastern Market meet is in the open, 7th & North Carolina Ave SE, ideal for covert photography establishing the relationship without active compromise.
